pr.title: ft: Match Popo (ftPp) and Kirby (ftKb) functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Matched a small set of fighter functions for Popo/Ice Climbers and Kirby. The Popo work implemented several SpecialHi-related helpers and enter/change-motion functions in ftPp_SpecialS.c, exposed needed declarations in ftPp_1211.h and ftPp_Init.h, and refined ftIceClimberAttributes with a typed float member at offset 0x84. The Kirby work matched ftKb_SpecialN_800F11AC, adding a forward declaration for fn_800F1CA0 and implementing a special-N cleanup path that calls ftKb_SpecialN_800EF69C with Kirby hat data and unloads dynamic bones. The PR was part of a split from PR #2024 and was verified by ninja build and SHA1 check.
what_changed: Implemented ftPp_SpecialHi_Enter in ftPp_SpecialS.c. The function gets the Fighter, reads ftIceClimberAttributes from fp->dat_attrs, divides fp->gr_vel by da->x84, calls ftPp_SpecialHi_801218AC, clears fp->cmd_vars[0..2], sets fp->mv.pp.unk_80123954.x0 to 1, clears fp->fv.pp.x223C, and zeroes fp->fv.pp.x2240 vector components.
Implemented ftPp_SpecialHi_801218AC as a motion-state transition to state 0x15B with Ft_MF_None followed by ftAnim_8006EBA4.
Implemented ftPp_SpecialHi_801218F8 as a motion-state transition to state 0x160 with Ft_MF_None followed by ftAnim_8006EBA4.
Implemented ftPp_SpecialHi_80121DA0 as a Fighter_ChangeMotionState call to state 0x15C.
Changed several Popo SpecialHi/SpecialLw IASA local declarations in ftPp_SpecialS.c from static to external-scope declarations, consistent with exposing or referencing non-static functions.
Included ftPopo/ftPp_1211.h from ftPp_SpecialS.c and added a declaration for ftPp_SpecialHi_80121DA0 to ftPp_1211.h.
Added extern f32 declarations for ftPp_Init_804D9860 and ftPp_Init_804D9880 in ftPp_Init.h.
Refined ftIceClimberAttributes in ftPopo/types.h by splitting padding around offset 0x84 and adding float x84.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F11AC in ftKb_Init.c. It reads Fighter* from gobj->user_data, calls ftKb_SpecialN_800EF69C(gobj, 0xF, ft_80459B88.hats[14]), then calls ftCo_UnloadDynamicBones(fp).
Added a static bool forward declaration for fn_800F1CA0(HSD_GObj* gobj) in ftKb_Init.c.
smart_moves: Converted a raw Ice Climbers dat_attrs offset access into a typed struct member, making the matched code cleaner and more reusable than pointer arithmetic.
Kept motion-state helper implementations minimal and idiomatic: direct Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls with observed constants, followed by ftAnim_8006EBA4 where required.
Added header declarations for functions and globals that were newly referenced across translation-unit boundaries instead of relying on undeclared or local-only symbols.

For Kirby, reused existing helper ftKb_SpecialN_800EF69C and the global hat table ft_80459B88.hats[14], then performed dynamic-bone cleanup through ftCo_UnloadDynamicBones, matching behavior without over-expanding unrelated logic.
decomp_lessons: When a matched function reveals a meaningful dat_attrs field, update the character attributes struct with a typed member at the exact offset rather than keeping casts such as *(float*)((u8*)da + 0x84).
Small character-function matches often require accompanying header cleanup: include the correct character header and add declarations for functions/globals that are now shared.
Motion-state wrapper functions can often be matched as thin calls to Fighter_ChangeMotionState with exact state IDs, flags, frame/rate/blend values, and NULL callbacks.

Clearing fighter command variables and per-character state fields in the same order as the target can matter for matching; this PR preserves explicit cmd_vars[2], [1], [0] writes and individual vector component zeroes.
Do not leave functions static if they need external declarations or are part of the character module’s exported symbol set; align linkage with how the function is referenced and declared.
Reviewer feedback can identify when an apparently local matching workaround should instead become a struct-layout improvement.
naming_conventions: Popo/Ice Climbers functions use the ftPp_ prefix, with action names such as SpecialHi, SpecialAirHi, SpecialHiThrow, SpecialLw, and suffixes like _Enter, _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll.
Unknown or not-yet-named functions retain address-based suffixes such as ftPp_SpecialHi_801218AC, ftPp_SpecialHi_801218F8, ftPp_SpecialHi_80121DA0, and ftKb_SpecialN_800F11AC.
Character attribute fields without semantic names are represented by offset-style names such as ftIceClimberAttributes.x84.
Global data declarations use address-style names such as ftPp_Init_804D9860 and ftPp_Init_804D9880.
Kirby functions use the ftKb_ prefix; common fighter helper calls retain subsystem prefixes such as ftCo_UnloadDynamicBones and Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace raw byte-offset loads from dat_attrs with a correctly placed float member in the character attributes struct to produce equivalent code with cleaner C.
Use explicit motion-state constants such as 0x15B, 0x160, and 0x15C when semantic enum names are not yet available or not evidenced.
Preserve apparent write ordering for cmd_vars and per-character state resets when matching initialization/enter functions.
Use Ft_MF_None for motion-state flags where the matched function requires a zero/no-flags motion-state change.
For Kirby SpecialN cleanup, obtain the Fighter through gobj->user_data, call the existing hat helper with hat index 14, and then unload dynamic bones.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Add a float-typed x84 member to ftIceClimberAttributes so ftPp_SpecialHi_Enter can use fp->gr_vel /= da->x84 instead of raw pointer arithmetic.

follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename ftIceClimberAttributes.x84 to a semantic field name.
Look for later matches of ftPp_SpecialAirHi_Enter, ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_Anim, ftPp_SpecialHi_80121D40, and ftPp_SpecialHi_80121DD8.
Search whether ftPp_Init_804D9860 and ftPp_Init_804D9880 are later typed, named, or moved into a data header.
Check later Kirby work around ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4, ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0, and fn_800F1CA0 for related matching patterns.
Review PR #2024 for the larger context that this PR was split from.
